# Internal Agent Governance Pack (Policy + Acknowledgments)
**Purpose:** To define internal agent conduct, commission rules, clawbacks, and conflicts management for Mi Casa Real Estate’s brokerage operations and to obtain agent acknowledgment for enforcement and audit.

## 1. Parties and Effective Date
Effective Date: ____ / ____ / ______  
Company: Mi Casa Real Estate (MiCasa Real Estate), CN-3762725, Abu Dhabi, UAE (the “Company”).  
Agent: __________________________ (the “Agent”)  
Agent ID (internal): __________________________  
Role: ☐ Sales ☐ Leasing ☐ Dual ☐ Team Lead ☐ Other: __________

## 2. Scope and Binding Nature
2.1 This Governance Pack sets binding internal rules for the Agent’s conduct and compensation arrangements while operating under the Company.  
2.2 This Governance Pack supplements the Agent’s employment/contractor agreement. If there is a conflict, the Company’s management will determine the applicable rule, subject to applicable law.

## 3. Code of Conduct
3.1 The Agent shall act honestly, professionally, and in good faith, and shall not mislead clients or other brokers.  
3.2 The Agent shall present property information accurately and must not publish false photos, false availability, or deceptive pricing.  
3.3 The Agent shall use only Company-approved marketing channels and materials and must ensure listing approvals are recorded.  
3.4 The Agent shall not accept cash, cheques, or transfers from clients unless expressly authorized by Company policy and documented approvals; all receipts must be issued through approved processes.  
3.5 The Agent shall keep complete and timely records in the CRM/ERP, including: client communications, viewing logs, offer history, approvals, and executed documents.

## 4. Confidentiality and Data Protection
4.1 The Agent shall protect confidential business and client information and shall not disclose it except for legitimate transaction purposes.  
4.2 The Agent shall process personal data only for Company business, only within authorized systems, and shall not export client lists, send bulk marketing without approvals, or store client IDs in personal devices without secure controls.  
4.3 The Agent must report immediately any suspected data breach, lost device, or accidental disclosure.

## 5. Conflicts of Interest
5.1 A conflict exists where the Agent’s personal interest could influence professional judgment (including dealing with relatives, personal ownership interests, side payments, or referral benefits).  
5.2 The Agent must disclose conflicts in writing to management before proceeding and must obtain written clearance.  
5.3 The Agent shall not represent both sides of the same transaction without full disclosure and written approvals in accordance with Company policy and applicable rules.

## 6. Commission Policy (Internal)
6.1 Commission is earned only when:  
(a) the Company has a signed authorization/representation on file covering the relevant party;  
(b) the transaction is executed (MOU/SPA/lease) and/or completed as required by Company policy; and  
(c) the Company has received and cleared the commission payment (unless management approves an exception in writing).

6.2 Commission splits are determined by the Company’s approved commission structure and the specific deal split sheet. No verbal agreements are valid unless approved in writing by management.  
6.3 The Company may place commissions on hold where:  
(a) there is a complaint or dispute;  
(b) compliance documentation is missing;  
(c) there is suspected misconduct, misrepresentation, or policy breach;  
(d) funds are subject to chargeback, refund requests, or non-clearance.

## 7. Clawbacks and Set-Off
7.1 If commission was paid to the Agent and later becomes refundable or is reversed due to:  
(a) transaction cancellation after payment;  
(b) client complaint upheld due to Agent misconduct;  
(c) breach of Company policy or unauthorized commitments;  
(d) fraud, falsified documents, or misrepresentation;  
then the Company may claw back the Agent’s paid commission and/or set it off against future earnings, subject to applicable law and documented calculations.

7.2 Clawback decisions must be documented with evidence and approved by management.

## 8. Non-Circumvention and Restrictive Conduct (Internal)
8.1 The Agent shall not divert Company leads, listings, or clients to personal channels or external agencies.  
8.2 The Agent shall not transact “off-book” or outside Company systems for any deal introduced or managed through the Company.

## 9. Disciplinary Process
9.1 Breaches may result in corrective actions including written warning, commission hold, clawback, suspension, termination, or legal action, depending on severity.  
9.2 The Agent will be given a reasonable opportunity to respond, except where urgent action is required to protect clients, funds, or compliance obligations.

## 10. Conflict of Interest Declaration (Agent)
The Agent declares as of the Effective Date:  
(a) ☐ No conflicts to disclose; or  
(b) ☐ Conflicts disclosed below and submitted for approval:  
Conflict Details:


## 11. Governing Law and Jurisdiction
This Governance Pack is governed by the laws of the United Arab Emirates and the Emirate of Abu Dhabi. The courts of Abu Dhabi shall have jurisdiction.

## 12. Entire Agreement (Internal Policy)
This Governance Pack constitutes the complete internal policy understanding between the Company and the Agent regarding the topics covered herein and supersedes prior internal instructions on the same topics, unless expressly stated otherwise in writing by management.
